title: pilosella arvicola subsp. subhyperborea
intro: Pilosella arvicola subsp. subhyperborea, also known as a subspecies of the plant Pilosella arvicola, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) native to the Arctic regions of Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as protect against cell damage. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, which may also contribute to its potential health benefits, such as improving cardiovascular health and reducing the risk of certain diseases. In herbal preparations, Pilosella arvicola subsp. subhyperborea is often used as a tincture or decoction, and may be combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and health benefits.
